name: Contradictory validation rules with stale API reference

planted_issues.description: Rule 1 requires thorough validation including external service calls (address, phone, email verification), but Rule 2 requires all responses within 200ms. External verification calls typically take 500ms-2s each. These two rules are mutually exclusive — the agent cannot satisfy both simultaneously.
Rule 3 references `api.v2.validate()` which was removed in the v3 migration. The current API is `api.v3.get_validation_message()`. Following this instruction will cause a runtime error.

No rule includes rationale or explains WHY it exists. Without context, an agent (or developer maintaining these rules) cannot resolve conflicts, make tradeoff decisions, or determine which rules take priority when they clash.

clean_areas.description: Rule 4 ('Log all authentication attempts with timestamp, IP address, and outcome') is clear, non-contradictory, and specifies exactly what to log and in what format.
